free_text: Fusion-fission events in the 11B + 181Ta reaction up
to 5.7 MeV/nucleon energy
14UD BARC-TIFR Pelletron facility
Self-supporting thin natural foils of Ta and Al
(purity 99.99%) having thickness 1.2 - 2.0 mg/cm2 and
1.5 - 2.0 mg/cm2, respectively, were prepared by
proper rolling technique.
The energy resolution of the detector was
2.0 keV at 1332 keV gamma rays of 60Co.
The average charge of 490 uC was collected
for each irradiation.
Total charge measured by an electron
suppressed Faraday cup installed behind the target
catcher.
Including error in the SRIM calculation and
target thickness determination.
The 95% confidence limit reported by the
authors.
The total uncertainty in the cross section are:
- Uncertainty in the estimation of beam energy due to
degradation of energy successive target-catcher foil.
- Energy straggling is expected to be small.
geometry-dependent efficiency of the
detector   (<= 2%)
non uniformity of target thickness  (2%)
incident-beam flux due to fluctuation in
beam current   (6-7%)
dead time of the detector (<= 7%)
Table III of Phys. Rev. C 103 (2021) 014608
